growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron is benefiting from a worsening AI-driven DRAM shortage and stronger memory pricing, with current market coverage pointing to a favorable memory cycle and continued demand from data centers.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap AI memory pure-play with severe DRAM/HBM shortage, blowout growth, large target upside, and supply tightness likely through 2027-2028.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: FIS has been cut roughly in half from its high yet still trades at about 8.1x earnings with an Aug. 4 print as the next hard catalyst; if the market has over-discounted execution risk, the valuation can re-rate, but payments competition and slower spending remain the bear case.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Deep-value payments infrastructure setup with real FCF, a low forward multiple, analyst upside, and fresh director buying after a large drawdown.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: A director bought about $500K of stock while the shares were still deeply out of favor, and follow-up coverage says CRM is the worst-performing Dow name in 2026 with a large buyback now in play.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Salesforce combines depressed valuation, strong cash flow, Agentforce momentum, insider buying, and a massive buyback.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Vertiv sits directly on the AI data-center bottleneck with power and liquid-cooling products, and its IR says Q2 2026 earnings are due July 29 while recent coverage keeps highlighting AI infrastructure demand.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Vertiv remains a top AI data-center power and cooling compounder with raised guidance and a near-term earnings catalyst, though valuation is no longer cheap.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Microsoft has pulled back sharply from its highs on AI capex worries, but the business is still a dominant cloud and software compounder with durable earnings power.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Microsoft is a high-quality cloud and software compounder priced for AI capex skepticism despite strong Azure and AI revenue momentum.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: China's rare-earth magnet exports to the U.S. are still running about 20% below pre-trade-war levels, and the White House's July 20, 2026 defense-supply-chain order explicitly pushes domestic critical-material sourcing, which tightens the moat around MP's Mountain Pass-to-magnet buildout.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Policy-backed domestic rare-earth supply chain leader with Pentagon capital support, large target upside, and a real August earnings catalyst.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Accenture raised fiscal 2026 repurchases by $2B to $7.5B and lifted total planned fiscal 2026 shareholder returns to $11.5B, a large capital-return signal from a mega-cap services franchise.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Accenture is a cash-rich services franchise trading below 10x forward earnings while aggressively pulling stock out of the market.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: UHS is 38.6% below its 52-week high, still only about 8% above the low, and reports on Jul. 27; the setup is a defensive healthcare name priced for reimbursement and labor risk, but the 6.3x P/E leaves room if margins stabilize.
growth_picks.analyst_take: UHS is a defensive healthcare operator at roughly 6x earnings with growing Q1 revenue and EPS, priced for reimbursement and labor stress.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: NATO's July 7, 2026 announcement to buy up to five MQ-4C Triton aircraft directly increases Northrop's ISR backlog and reinforces demand for maritime surveillance as alliance tensions stay elevated.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Northrop has a fresh guidance raise, backlog growth, and NATO Triton demand while trading well below its high.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ServiceNow has a near-term Q2 earnings date on July 22 and is pitching AI governance and Control Tower as the enterprise moat, which matters as buyers move from experimentation to governed workflows.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-margin workflow compounder reset to a reasonable forward multiple, with AI governance and Now Assist providing a credible enterprise monetization path.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Hut 8 finalized a 15-year, $9.8 billion lease at Beacon Point in Texas, doubling a tenant's committed capacity to 704 MW and pushing the campus toward full commercialization, which turns the company into a contracted AI data-center landlord rather than a speculative miner remnant.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Beacon Point's long-duration lease turns Hut 8 into a contracted AI infrastructure landlord, but losses and execution timing keep it speculative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Tyler Technologies is roughly 50% below its 52-week high despite a profitable Q1 2026 with GAAP net income of $81.2M and ARR growth of 10.4%, and it reports on July 29, 2026; the bear case is that the cloud transition is good but not good enough to re-rate the stock quickly.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Tyler is a profitable public-sector SaaS compounder trading nearly 49% below its high with a near-term earnings catalyst and meaningful target upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The Department of War awarded Lockheed a $439.4M ATACMS missile contract for Taiwan on July 15, 2026, and Lockheed also announced an $850M Trident II D5 modernization award on July 10, reinforcing near-term demand from Indo-Pacific deterrence and strategic nuclear recapitalization.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lockheed has defense backlog, missile demand, and a near-term earnings catalyst, with valuation reasonable after the drawdown.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: NVIDIA's July 20 Schedule 13G shows 22,256,412 NBIS shares, including 1,190,476 direct shares and 21,065,936 shares via a prefunded warrant, which is about a 9.3% stake and roughly $2B of strategic capital behind Nebius's AI infrastructure buildout.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Nvidia's disclosed 9.3% stake validates Nebius as a strategic AI infrastructure platform, but the stock remains a high-burn speculative growth name.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Barclays upgraded Lumentum on the view that AI networking demand and laser shortages are still tightening the market, with management saying products are sold out through 2027 and margin gains already showing up in the numbers.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lumentum has real AI optical-networking scarcity and sold-out demand, but the valuation leaves little room for disappointment.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Fiserv is about 69% below its 52-week high and only about 10% above its low, but the CEO exit has already triggered insider buying and fresh strategic-interest chatter ahead of its Aug. 6 earnings release; the bear case is that the payments reset proves structural rather than temporary.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Fiserv is a deeply reset payments franchise with insider buying after the CEO exit and an August earnings catalyst.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: HII is 41.3% below its high and within 8% of the low, with Jul. 30 earnings as the near-term checkpoint; the upside is defense backlog and shipbuilding execution, while program delays or budget noise are the bear case.
growth_picks.analyst_take: HII offers shipbuilding backlog value near the lows, but margin compression and negative Q1 free cash flow keep conviction below the prime defense names.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Comcast combines a low valuation with a near-52-week-low setup and an earnings date on July 23 where options imply a move, creating a classic contrarian reset candidate.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Energy Fuels received conditional U.S. government support in July 2026 to accelerate rare-earth and critical-materials processing, a direct policy-backed beneficiary of the U.S. effort to rebuild domestic industrial capacity.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: AMD is catching a meaningful reset after the semiconductor bear-market selloff, but the expanded Microsoft partnership gives the stock a fresh fundamental backstop.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Microsoft Helios/Azure validation is real, but the stock already prices in a lot of AI success with limited target upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Astera Labs posted 93% year-over-year Q1 revenue growth, is shipping its Scorpio AI fabric switch family, and has a Q2 earnings date in early August, which keeps the rack-scale AI connectivity thesis alive.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Astera has excellent AI connectivity growth, but extreme valuation, negative target upside, and insider selling leave little margin for error.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Datadog is turning AI observability into a real product layer through its Adaptive ML acquisition, Bits AI launches, and GPU monitoring tools, which should keep it embedded in production AI operations.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: PayPal is still about 30% below its 52-week high after the Stripe/Advent takeover report, and the July 28, 2026 earnings release is a concrete catalyst for the turnaround and restructuring story; the bear case is that the bid never closes and the stock reverts to slow-growth payments multiple hell.
growth_picks.analyst_take: PayPal is cheap and has a takeover catalyst, but the reported bid is close to the current price and analyst targets remain below market.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Manhattan Associates is about one-third below its 52-week high even though Q1 2026 revenue and EPS beat and Q2 results land on July 28, 2026; the bear case is that investors keep paying for 'good' software only after the next print proves the slowdown was temporary.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Manhattan is a quality supply-chain software name with a Q2 catalyst, but valuation and limited target upside cap risk/reward.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Paycom is still about 40% below its 52-week high, but Q1 2026 produced $155.7M of net income and management continued heavy buybacks, with Q2 results due August 5, 2026; the bear case is that the market keeps punishing the multiple if revenue growth stays only mid-teens or below.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Paycom is cheap for its margins and buyback support, but slow growth and negative target upside make it a wait-for-proof turnaround.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Energy has been the strongest sector in 2026, and Exxon remains one of the core large-cap beneficiaries of that rotation with additional power-demand leverage.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: General Dynamics Electric Boat received a July 9, 2026 $255M modification for operational strategic and attack submarine support, adding to an already submarine-heavy backlog just as allied undersea deterrence demand remains elevated.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: EIA reported record 2025 global LNG trade, 26% U.S. LNG export growth, and continued 2026-2027 export gains as a key Qatar export route was disrupted, a direct structural positive for Cheniere's Gulf Coast liquefaction and export footprint.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Yahoo Finance highlighted a QXO call-ratio backspread that netted a trader about a $468k credit, which is the kind of large, asymmetric options structure I treat as informed positioning ahead of a potential catalyst.
growth_picks.analyst_take: QXO has a scaled acquisition platform and huge analyst upside, but the pro forma story still needs clean execution proof after Beacon, Kodiak, and TopBuild.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Freeport is benefiting from a fresh copper pullback-and-rebound setup, and Goldman’s M&A-candidate framing keeps it on the radar as a high-beta materials play tied to AI infrastructure and electrification.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: RTX said on July 7, 2026 that Raytheon is doubling global Stinger production and expanding its European supplier base, a direct beneficiary of NATO's push to harden short-range air defense capacity.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX is a high-quality defense and aerospace compounder with real Stinger and engine catalysts, but valuation and limited upside argue against chasing.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: IREN signed $2.8 billion of new multiyear AI cloud contracts, lifted its year-end AI cloud revenue target above $4 billion, and said customer prepayments will cover a large share of GPU capex, making the neocloud pivot look materially de-risked.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Oracle has been hit hard, trading near its lowest level since June 2024 after a steep monthly slide, yet it still has meaningful AI supply-deal optionality.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Oracle looks optically cheap after a collapse, but negative FCF, extreme leverage, credit downgrade risk, and AI customer concentration violate the quality screen.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: PPC sits 39.8% below its high but only 9.9% above the low, with a Jul. 30 earnings call and a sub-8x P/E; low float and 15.6% short interest can help if chicken spreads hold, but commodity volatility and disease headlines are the bear case.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Pilgrim's Pride is optically cheap, but Q1 profit fell sharply, free cash flow is negative, and the target upside is too small for commodity risk.
